title: Encoders, Decoders, and Multiplexers
summary: Understand programmable logic circuits that route and encode data: encoders, decoders, and MUX/DEMUX.
content_html:
Beyond basic gates and adders, digital systems use more complex combinational building blocks for data routing and encoding.

Encoder

An encoder converts a one-hot input (only one line active) to a binary code. A 4-to-2 encoder has 4 inputs and 2 binary output bits:

I0=1 → Y=00
I1=1 → Y=01
I2=1 → Y=10
I3=1 → Y=11

Decoder

A decoder does the reverse: converts a binary code to one-hot output. A 2-to-4 decoder with inputs A, B activates one of 4 output lines.

Multiplexer (MUX)

A MUX selects one of N inputs to pass to a single output based on select lines. A 4-to-1 MUX needs 2 select lines (S1, S0):

S1=0, S0=0 → output = I0
S1=0, S0=1 → output = I1
S1=1, S0=0 → output = I2
S1=1, S0=1 → output = I3

Demultiplexer (DEMUX)

A DEMUX takes one input and routes it to one of N outputs based on select lines — the reverse of a MUX.

Programmable Logic

Devices like PLDs (Programmable Logic Devices) allow custom Boolean functions to be implemented in reconfigurable hardware.

exercises.title: Encoder function
options: Converts one-hot input to binary code
Converts binary code to one-hot output
exercises.answer: Converts one-hot input to binary code

exercises.title: Decoder function
exercises.prompt: A 2-to-4 decoder with input 10₂ activates which output line?

exercises.title: MUX purpose
exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a multiplexer?
options: To select one of many inputs and route it to a single output
exercises.answer: To select one of many inputs and route it to a single output

exercises.title: 4-to-1 MUX select lines
exercises.prompt: How many select lines does a 4-to-1 MUX need?

exercises.title: DEMUX
options: The reverse of a MUX — routes one input to one of many outputs
exercises.answer: The reverse of a MUX — routes one input to one of many outputs

exercises.title: Encoder input style
exercises.prompt: An encoder typically expects what type of input?
options: One-hot (only one active line at a time)
exercises.answer: One-hot (only one active line at a time)

exercises.title: Select lines for 8-to-1
exercises.prompt: How many select lines are needed for an 8-to-1 MUX?

exercises.title: 4-to-2 encoder outputs
exercises.prompt: A 4-to-2 encoder produces how many output bits?

exercises.title: MUX select
exercises.prompt: In a 4-to-1 MUX, if S1=1 and S0=0, which input is selected?
